title: Lattice Dashboard
description: Top-level schema for a Lattice dashboard document: a manifest plus a single root item instance forming a recursive tree.

root.description: The root item instance. Conventionally a container, but the container-only-children rule is enforced by the resolver (E1-S4), not the schema.

variables.description: Document-scope variable declarations. Visible to every node unless shadowed by a same-named declaration on a descendant container (E3-S1).

theme.description: Optional document-scope DEFAULT THEME (E2-S2): the document's base presentation choices, drawn from the shared theme vocabulary. Its value is constrained to the theme schema's semantic tokens, so an out-of-vocabulary token (or value) is rejected by structural validation. The resolver attaches it verbatim to the resolved document output as the default layer; per-block theme overrides (E2-S3) are emitted side-by-side and are NOT merged here.

connections.description: Optional document-scoped data connection instances. Each references a connection-type schema in schemas/connections/ via $ref; the resolver validates each connection's config against that type (E4-S1). Connections are declared and validated only, never dialed.

varDeclaration.title: Variable Declaration
varDeclaration.description: A typed variable declaration { name, type, default?|expr?, options? }. Declarable at document scope and on container instances; resolution walks the tree with inner-shadows-outer semantics (E3-S1). A variable's value is either a literal default or a computed expr (E3-S3), never both.

name.description: Variable identifier, unique within its declaring scope.

default.description: Optional default value; validated against type by the resolver (E3-S1). Mutually exclusive with expr.

expr.description: Optional computed-variable expression (github.com/expr-lang/expr) evaluated against the in-scope variables; its result is coerced/validated to type by the resolver (E3-S3). Mutually exclusive with default.

options.description: Permitted values for enum-typed variables. Required for enum, forbidden otherwise; enforced by the resolver (E3-S1).

connection.title: Connection Instance
connection.description: A document-scoped data connection instance. References a connection-type schema via $ref and carries opaque per-connection config validated against that type by the resolver (E4-S1). Secrets are referenced indirectly via secretRefs, never inlined.

id.description: Connection identifier, unique within the document. Items bind to a connection by this id.

$ref.description: URI of the connection-type schema this connection is an instance of (e.g. https://lattice.dev/schemas/connections/static/1.0.0). Validated against the catalog by the resolver.

config.description: Per-connection configuration. Structure is defined by the referenced connection-type's schema; opaque at the dashboard level.

secretRefs.description: Optional indirection from a config slot to a named secret resolved out-of-band. Maps a logical secret name to an opaque reference token; secret values are never inlined in the document.

formatVersion.description: Semantic version of the dashboard document format this document targets.

id.description: Stable, machine-readable identifier for the dashboard.

description.description: Optional longer-form description of the dashboard.

author.description: Optional author or owner of the dashboard.

instance.title: Item Instance
instance.description: A recursive instance node. References an item type via $ref, carries opaque per-instance config and placement, and may declare children. Whether children are permitted depends on the referenced item type (container-only); that rule is enforced by the resolver in E1-S4, not structurally here.

$ref.description: URI of the item-type schema this instance is an instance of (e.g. https://lattice.dev/schemas/items/table/1.0.0). Validated against the catalog by the resolver.

id.description: Optional instance-local identifier, unique within the document.

config.description: Per-instance configuration. Structure is defined by the referenced item type's schema; opaque at the dashboard level.

placement.description: Optional layout placement hints interpreted by the parent container's grid. Minimal/stubbed here; E2-S1 formalizes track placement.

children.description: Optional child instances. Structurally permitted on any instance; only valid when the referenced type is a container, enforced by the resolver in E1-S4.

variables.description: Optional variable declarations scoped to this instance and its descendants. Meaningful on container instances; an inner declaration shadows a same-named outer one (E3-S1).
